prompt: The volatile variable still has a race condition. What is the bug?
choices: volatile fields cannot be incremented with ++
volatile ensures visibility but not atomicity; count++ is read-modify-write and is not atomic, so two threads can both read the same value and both write the incremented value, losing an update
volatile variables must be declared static
count++ is thread-safe for volatile int fields
explanation: volatile guarantees that reads/writes are visible to all threads (no caching), but does not make compound operations atomic. count++ = read count + increment + write count (three operations). Two threads can interleave: both read 5, both write 6, losing one increment. Fix: use AtomicInteger.incrementAndGet() or synchronized.
